expectations.evidence: File exists at components/common/reviewCard/index.tsx with a fully implemented ReviewCard component.
Component is created at reviewCard/index.tsx, following the directory/index.tsx pattern.

Component is in reviewCard/index.tsx (directory-based), not a flat ReviewCard.tsx file. Note: the folder name uses camelCase (reviewCard) rather than PascalCase (ReviewCard), but the structural pattern is correct.
The component is exported as 'ReviewCard' (PascalCase) on line 14 and line 102.
Line 3: 'export interface ReviewCardProps' follows the {ComponentName}Props naming convention.
Lines 36-49: Avatar element with data-testid='review-card-avatar' renders either an img (when avatarUrl is provided) or initials fallback.
Lines 59-79: Star rating element with data-testid='review-card-rating' renders 5 SVG stars, colored based on the rating value.
Lines 84-89: Review body paragraph with data-testid='review-card-body' renders the body prop.
Lines 92-97: Date paragraph with data-testid='review-card-date' renders the createdAt prop.
Tailwind classes used extensively throughout: flex, gap-3, rounded-xl, border, bg-white, text-sm, font-semibold, dark: variants, etc.

expectations.description: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Grep for useEffect|fetch|axios|useQuery|useMutation across all output files returned no matches. The component is purely presentational.

summary: All 11 expectations passed. The ReviewCard component is a well-structured, presentational component following the directory/index.tsx pattern with PascalCase naming, proper Props interface, and all required UI elements (avatar, star rating, review body, date). It uses Tailwind CSS for styling and contains no data-fetching logic.
eval_feedback: The agent produced a clean, idiomatic ReviewCard component that satisfies all requirements. The component handles avatar fallback gracefully with initials, implements an SVG-based star rating system, and includes proper dark mode support. Minor note: the folder name uses camelCase (reviewCard) rather than PascalCase (ReviewCard), but the component name itself is PascalCase as required.
